notes.total: 171 tickets scoped across 7 sprints is a healthy volume for the quarter, averaging ~24 tickets per sprint with no signs of overload or underutilisation.
notes.completed: 170 of 171 tickets completed — only 1 ticket remained incomplete at quarter close, demonstrating excellent delivery execution.
notes.completion_rate: 99% completion rate is outstanding and reflects strong sprint discipline and minimal unfinished work at quarter end.
notes.releases_shipped: 24 releases shipped across 7 sprints (3.4 average, 3 median per sprint) demonstrates excellent release cadence and consistent deployment rhythm.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint tickets added — perfect sprint planning discipline with no reactive mid-sprint scope changes.
notes.oos_open: Zero open OOS items — no unplanned debt carried forward.
notes.type_split: 18% bugs (31 of 171) is a healthy ratio, with the bulk of work focused on stories (50) and tasks (90), indicating stable product quality.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 3 releases per closed sprint reflects strong, consistent deployment cadence with no sprints shipping nothing.
notes.time_logged: Only 348 hours logged against 2,511 hours estimated (14% logged) indicates severe time-tracking gaps — logging compliance is critically poor across the team.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 14% estimate accuracy (86% negative variance) is misleading due to poor logging — only 45 tickets logged time, making variance calculation unreliable.
notes.no_estimate: 45 tickets (26%) completed without estimates reduces planning reliability, though impact is moderate given strong delivery outcomes.
notes.rollover: Only 1 ticket rolled over (1% of total) — exceptional sprint predictability with minimal carryover between sprints.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 15 days is reasonable for the quarter, though mean of 25 days suggests some longer-running tickets inflated the average.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload is well-distributed with no single person over 22% of total tickets (Ivor at 34, Riccardo at 38 across extended team), though 4 of 8 contributors logged zero hours indicating time-tracking is not enforced consistently.
